scheme_name: Scholarship for Ph.D. Students of Socially and Educationally Backward Classes
page_title: Scholarship for Ph.D. Students of Socially and Educationally Backward Classes

details: The “Scholarship for Ph.D. Students of Socially and Educationally Backward Classes” scheme was launched by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) students pursuing M.Phil. or Ph.D. to encourage higher education and research.
eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should belong to the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC).
The applicant should be pursuing M.Phil or Ph.D at a recognized institution.
The applicant’s annual family income should not exceed ₹6,00,000/-.

online: The applicant may visit the Digital Gujarat Portal:
On the home page, click on ‘Register’ and the applicant will be redirected to the registration form page.
Enter your Mobile Number, Email, Set Password, and then click on ‘Save’.
After successful registration, the applicant can login through his/her User Name (Mobile No./Email ID) and Password.
Now, click on ‘My Profile’ to update your profile and fill in all the mandatory information.
After filling in profile details, click on ‘Update Profile’. The profile will be updated after entering the verification Code (OTP).
Now under services, select ‘Scholarship Services’ and then click on ‘SC Scheme’.
Select the Financial Year for which you want to apply for and then click on ‘Change Financial Year’.
Select the scheme, read the interaction carefully, and then click on ‘Continue to Service’.
Fill in the application form completely and upload all the relevant documents.
Now, click on ‘Save as Draft’ and agree with the terms & conditions, and submit the application form by clicking on ‘Confirm & Final Submit’.
Photo uploading for all applicants is mandatory.
Application will be considered complete only when all sections are completed.

Only one application per applicant will be acceptable for a scholarship. If any student submits more than one application either from a single institute or from multiple institutions his/her all applications will be rejected by the competent authority and no request from any side will be entertained.
In case of any wrong/misleading information provided in the application shall lead to rejection of the application by Department Authorities.
All required documents must be carefully scanned as per the instructions given.
The student has to fill his/her application carefully as there is no provision for institution change once the application is submitted successfully.
The unreadable uploaded documents will allow the competent authority to reject the student application.

Direct Individual submission of this application form will not be accepted in case of students studying inside the state. Only outside students have to fill out their form online and submit his/her application form to their native district officer.

documents_required: Other Mark sheets/Last Year Mark sheet

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students belonging to socially and educationally backward classes who are domiciled in Gujarat and pursuing M.Phil. or Ph.D. are eligible.

faqs.question: What is the benefit amount for M.Phil. students?
faqs.answer: Eligible M.Phil. students will receive ₹25,000/- as a scholarship.

faqs.question: Can students from other states apply?
faqs.answer: No, only students domiciled in Gujarat are eligible.

faqs.question: How do I track my application?
faqs.answer: Use the application/reference number on the Digital Gujarat portal: https://www.digitalgujarat.gov.in .

faqs.question: Is there an income limit for eligibility?
faqs.answer: Family income must not exceed ₹6,00,000 per annum.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply?
faqs.answer: Bank Passbook/Cancelled Cheque, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Marksheet, Receipt of fee paid, etc.

faqs.question: What is the benefit amount for Ph.D. students?
faqs.answer: Eligible Ph.D. students will receive a stipend of ₹1,00,000/- for thesis preparation.

faqs.question: Can part-time Ph.D. students apply?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is applicable only for full-time research scholars.

faqs.question: Is there any reservation within this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is exclusively for socially and educationally backward classes.
